title: 2010_instability_control_by_premixed_pilot_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content, extracted from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ΦPF1 = 1.1  (Phi_PFI = 1.1)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Investigated cases without pilot ring injection. All cases with PFI injection were conducted with 7.5 kg/h PFI air mass flow rate at ΦPF1=1.1.**

    | Cases        | Preheat temperature (K) | Outlet diameter (mm) | PFI injection (-) |
    | ------------ | ----------------------- | -------------------- | ----------------- |
    | Baseline-550 K | 550                     | 200                | No                |
    | PFI-550 K    | 550                     | 200                | Yes               |
    | PFI-550 K-65 mm | 550                     | 65                 | Yes               |
    | PFI-300 K    | 300                     | 200                | Yes               |

*   **Table 2: Investigated cases with pilot ring injection. All test cases were conducted at a main air preheat temperature of 550 K.**

    | Cases           | PFI air (kg/h) | PFI fuel (kg/h) | PR air (kg/h) | PR fuel (kg/h) |
    | --------------- | ------------- | -------------- | ------------- | -------------- |
    | Baseline        | 1             | 0             | 0             | 0             |
    | Premixed ring   | 1             | 0             | 15            | 0.96           |
    | Diffusion ring  | 1             | 0             | 0             | 0.96           |
    | Air ring        | 1             | 0             | 15            | 0               |

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 2: PFI installation at the cone vertex and Pilot Ring at the combustor's dump plane. Insert shows the prototype of the PFI:**
    *   Components/Labels:
        *   Central Recirculation Zone
        *   Side Recirculation Zone
        *   Pilot ring
        *   Pilot flame
        *   Swirl burner
        *   PFI lance
        *   Spark plug
        *   Fuel/Air (streams feeding the lance)
        *   Fuel/Air (streams in 'A-A' section)
        *    (1) sparkplug
        *    (2) ground electrode
        *    (3) ceramic tube

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Main air mass flow rate: 200 kg/h
*   Main air temperatures: 300 K (nonpreheated), 550 K (preheated)
*   Acoustic reflection coefficient: r (varies, r < 0.3)
*   Outlet diameter: 200 mm (full reflecting), 65 mm (low reflection configuration)
*   Pilot air mass flow rate: 7.5 kg/h
*   Spark Frequency: 110 Hz
*   Pilot Ring Diameter: 110 mm
*   Pilot Ring tube diameter: 6 mm
*   Fuel injector diameter: 1 mm
*   Bandpass filter: 312 +- 2 nm
*   ICCD camera distance 1.4 m
*

title: 2010_instability_control_by_premixed_pilot_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the breakdown of the technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   R = integral from 0 to t of (p'(t) * (OH)'(t))dt > dissipation losses (1)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 3: Stability map of a swirl-stabilized combustion**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Total equivalence ratio (Φtotal)
        *   Y-axis: Normalized RMS pressure level
    *   Curves:
        *   Baseline - 550K
        *   PFI - 550K
        *   PFI - 550K - 65mm
        *   PFI - 300K
    * Key values:
         * Baseline - 550K reaches a max normalized RMS pressure level of 17 at total equivelence ratio of about 0.6
         * All curves start at total equivelence ratio of 0.4

*   **Fig. 4: OH-chemiluminescence and pressure signals**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Time [sec]
        *   Y-axis: Amplitude [Volt]
    *   Curves:
        *   Microphone
        *   OH-signal
    * Key values:
         *  Microphone signal has a frequency of about 110Hz (1/0.009 sec)

*   **Fig. 5: Power spectrum**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Frequency [Hz]
        *   Y-axis: Power spectrum
    *   Curves:
        *   PFI flame off
        *   PFI flame
    * Key values:
         * PFI flame off curve shows a larger spike near 110 Hz than PFI flame curve

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Preheat air temperatures: 300 K and 550 K
*   Combustion outlet diameters: 65 mm and 200 mm
*   Low frequency pressure oscillations: 3-7 Hz
*   Fundamental frequency: 110 Hz
*   Phase-averaged OH images recorded in 45 deg steps
*   PFI air mass flow rate: 7.5 kg/h
*   ΦpF = 1.1 (phi PFI)
*   Φtotal ≤ 0.47 (baseline-550 K)
*   0.47 ≤ Φtotal ≤ 0.6 (PFI-550 K, thermo-acoustic instability)
*   Φtotal > 0.6 (PFI-550 K, stable combustion)
*   Φtotal ≥ 0.6 (highest heat release rate detected within shear layer)
*   Φtotal ≤ 0.5 (highest heat release rate approx. one combustor diameter down stream)
*   Φtotal = 0.55 (Fig. 4 PFI-550 K)
*   Φtotal = 0.55 (Fig 5 PFI-550 K)
*   Φtotal = 0.66 (PFI-300 K)
*   Φtotal > 0.67 (PFI-300 K, unstable)

title: 2010_instability_control_by_premixed_pilot_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Φtotal = 0.66
*   Φtotal = 0.45
*   Φtotal = 0.55
*   Φtotal = 0.58

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 7:**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: Normalized RMS pressure level
        *   X-axis: Total equivalence ratio Φtotal
    *   Units: No explicit units provided for Normalized RMS pressure level; Total equivalence ratio is dimensionless.
    *   Curves: Two curves are present
        *   Lean-Rich
        *   Rich-Lean
    *   Key values - approximate values read from the graph:
        *   Lean-Rich starts from approximately 0.45 at value 2 and ends at 0.7 at same value 2.
        *   Rich-Lean starts at approximately 0.45 at value 4,  reaches peak value of 18 at around 0.58 equivalence ratio, and then drops back to 2 at 0.68.

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 6 (Lower Right inset):**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Swirl burner (top view)
        *   ICCD Camera
        *   Angle indicated as 45 degrees
        *   1400mm indicator/dimension

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Main air mass flow rate: 200 kg/h.
*   Nonpreheated air temperature: 300 K.
*   Instability frequency: 82 Hz.
*   Baseline temperature: 550 K.
*   Time duration for PFI spark-off: 12 s (initial)
*   Time duration for stable combustion after PFI spark-off: approx 6 s
*   Time to re-activate spark-plug: after 56 s

title: 2010_instability_control_by_premixed_pilot_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   No explicit equations are provided. Some parameters are used in the x axis of the graphs.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 13: Influence of the pilot ring flames (diffusion ring and premixed ring) on the normalized rms pressure level.**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Total equivalence ratio (ΦTotal)
        *   y-axis: Normalized RMS pressure level
    *   Units:
        *   Total equivalence ratio: dimensionless
        *   Normalized RMS pressure level: dimensionless
    *   Curves:
        *   Baseline (solid line with circle markers)
        *   Diffusion Ring (dashed line with square markers)
        *   Premixed Ring (dotted line with triangle markers)
        *   Air Ring (solid line with diamond shape markers)
    *   Key Values:
        *   ΦTotal ranges from 0.42 to 0.62
        *   All RMS values were normalized on the background noise of the baseline case at ΦTotal = 0.45

*   **Fig. 14: NOx emission for different pilot ring injection cases.**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Total equivalence ratio (ΦTotal)
        *   y-axis: NOx [ppm] (dry, 15% O2)
    *   Units:
        *   Total equivalence ratio: dimensionless
        *   NOx: ppm (parts per million)
    *   Curves:
        *   Baseline (solid line with circle markers)
        *   Diffusion Ring (dashed line with square markers)
        *   Premixed Ring (dotted line with triangle markers)
        *   Air Ring (solid line with diamond shape markers)
    *   Key Values:
        *   ΦTotal ranges from 0.42 to 0.62

**3. Tables**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams**

*   **Fig. 15: Mean flame heat release of the four injection configurations depending on the total equivalence ratio Φtotal**
    *   Components and labels:
        *   Images show flame shapes for different total equivalence ratios (ΦTotal) and injection configurations. These are qualitative rather than precisely labeled diagrams.
        *  ΦTotal = 0.47,  ΦTotal = 0.50,  ΦTotal = 0.55,  ΦTotal = 0.6
        *   Injection configurations: Baseline, Diffusion ring, Premixed ring, Air ring

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   ΦTotal (Total equivalence ratio)
*   RMS pressure level
*   Baseline case at ΦTotal=0.45
*   15 kg/h air mass flow rate
*   NOx emission (ppm)
* Phi_main of the main flame

title: 2010_instability_control_by_premixed_pilot_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here is the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 16:**
    *   X-axis: Main equivalence ratio, Φmain
    *   Y-axis: Normalized RMS pressure level deviation
    *   Curves:
        *   Diffusion Ring: Starts at (0.35, ~5.5) and drops sharply to (0.5, ~4), then decreases slightly.
        *   Baseline: Relatively constant around -1 to -2 from 0.45 to 0.65.
        *   Premixed Ring: Around 2 at φmain =0.45 and decreases to -3 at φmain=0.5 and stays almost the same.
        *   Air Ring: Slightly positive around -3 at φmain =0.55 and then drops down below -5.

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   **Nomenclature:**
    *   TCCW = wall temperature of the combustion chamber
    *   OH* = OH-chemiluminescence
    *   Φtotal = total equivalence ratio calculated from Φmain and ΦPFI
    *   Φmain = main equivalence ratio of the main combustion
    *   ΦPFI = equivalence ratio of the flame produced by the pilot flame injector
    *   Φpilot = equivalence ratio of the pilot flame
    *   p' = pressure fluctuation within the combustion chamber
    *   R = Rayleigh Index
*   Φmain ≈ 0.51
